title: Aziz Hashim Biography
description: What are the professional background and current positions of Aziz Hashim?
narrative: The user seeks a concise overview of Aziz Hashim, emphasizing his executive roles, business ventures, and notable affiliations. Relevant information includes his titles such as President and Secretary of Frisch's Restaurants, Founder and Managing Partner of NRD Capital Management, CEO of NRD Partners I, and ownership of Rally's burger franchises, as well as any other publicly documented responsibilities.

title: Rep Scalise
description: Who is Representative Steve Scalise?
narrative: The user seeks a concise biographical overview of U.S. House Representative Steve Scalise, including his political role (e.g., party affiliation, leadership positions such as House Whip), the district he represents, and basic personal information. Relevant documents should provide details about his congressional duties, the geographic area of his district, and any notable personal background.

title: Nicholas II Killer
description: Who was responsible for the death of Tsar Nicholas II of Russia?
narrative: The user wants reliable historical information identifying the person(s) or group that ordered and carried out the execution of Nicholas II in July 1918, including any named perpetrators (e.g., Bolshevik officials, execution squad members) and supporting evidence of their involvement.

title: Monk Theme Singer
description: Who performed (sang) the theme song for the TV series *Monk*?
narrative: The user is looking for the name of the vocalist associated with the *Monk* theme song, specifically the version that includes vocals (the later‑season theme 'It’s a Jungle Out There'). Relevant documents should explicitly mention the singer’s name (e.g., Randy Newman) and tie it to the *Monk* series or its soundtrack. Sources that only discuss instrumental versions or unrelated soundtracks are not relevant.

title: Shotgun Patterning
description: Why do hunters pattern their shotguns and how does choke selection influence the pattern?
narrative: The user wants an explanation of the purpose of shotgun patterning for hunting, including how different choke types (e.g., cylinder, improved cylinder, modified) affect shot spread and effectiveness for various game. Relevant documents should describe the reasons hunters perform patterns, the relationship between choke constriction and pattern density, and guidance on selecting chokes for specific hunting scenarios.

title: Pete Rose Ban
description: Explain why Pete Rose was banned from the Baseball Hall of Fame.
narrative: The user wants to understand the reasons behind Pete Rose's exclusion from the Hall of Fame, focusing on his gambling on baseball and the lifetime ban imposed by MLB; documents that discuss his betting scandal, the MLB ban, and the ongoing debate over his Hall of Fame eligibility are considered relevant.

title: Geon Definition
description: What does the term 'geon' mean in cognitive psychology and visual perception?
narrative: The user is looking for a concise definition of 'geon', the basic geometric shape primitive proposed by psychologists to explain how humans decompose visual stimuli. Relevant documents should clearly explain that geons are simple 3D shapes used in object recognition theory, provide the definition, and may give examples or context about their role in shape perception.

title: Pareto Chart Definition
description: What is a Pareto chart and how is it used in statistical analysis?
narrative: The user seeks a concise, authoritative definition of a Pareto chart, including its visual components (ordered bars and cumulative line), its purpose for identifying the most significant factors in a data set, and its connection to the Pareto principle. Relevant documents should explicitly describe the chart, provide examples of construction and interpretation, and discuss typical applications such as quality control or root‑cause analysis.

title: Clothes Whitening
description: What are the most effective methods to make white clothes brighter and keep them white?
narrative: The user seeks practical, proven techniques for restoring and maintaining the whiteness of laundry. Relevant material should describe step‑by‑step procedures, ingredients or products (e.g., bleach, baking soda, detergents), effectiveness, safety considerations, and any tips for preventing future yellowing or discoloration.

title: Radio Wave Medium
description: What medium, if any, do radio waves travel through?
narrative: The user is seeking a clear explanation of the propagation medium for radio waves, specifically whether they require a material substance or can propagate through vacuum. Relevant results should describe radio waves as electromagnetic waves that can travel in a vacuum, clarify that a vacuum acts as a transmission medium in this context, and contrast this with wave types that need a material medium.

title: UN FAO Definition
description: What is the United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization and what are its primary roles?
narrative: The user is looking for a concise definition of the UN FAO, including its relationship to the United Nations and a summary of its main responsibilities such as combating hunger, addressing undernourishment, supporting rural employment, and improving food systems. Relevant documents should explicitly mention the FAO as a UN agency, describe its mission, and outline its key programs and activities related to nutrition, agriculture, and food security.

title: Reba Net Worth
description: What is the current net worth of country singer Reba McEntire?
narrative: The user wants an up-to-date estimate of Reba McEntire's net worth, including the dollar amount and the source of the figure. Relevant documents should provide a clear monetary value, the date of the estimate, and cite a reputable financial or entertainment source (e.g., Forbes, Celebrity Net Worth, Bloomberg).

title: Chaff and Flare
description: What are chaff and flare, and what are their purposes and typical uses?
narrative: The user wants a clear definition and overview of chaff and flare, including how each device works, their common military and civilian applications (e.g., signaling, illumination, defensive countermeasures), and handling or loading restrictions. Relevant content should describe the nature of these pyrotechnic or countermeasure cartridges, their functions, and any operational guidelines.

title: Geologic Nonconformity
description: Define a nonconformity in the context of earth science/geology.
narrative: The user wants a concise, authoritative definition of a nonconformity as used in geology, including its key characteristics, how it differs from other unconformities (e.g., disconformities), and illustrative examples of where such surfaces occur in the sedimentary record.

title: Oklahoma Workplace Discrimination
description: What steps can someone take to combat discrimination at work in Oklahoma City?
narrative: The user seeks practical guidance on addressing workplace discrimination in Oklahoma City, including legal rights under federal and state law, procedures for filing complaints with agencies such as the EEOC or Oklahoma Human Rights Commission, available local advocacy resources, and employer‑level remedies. Relevant material should explain what discrimination is, cite applicable statutes, and detail actionable options for victims.

title: Carnitine Precursor
description: Which amino acid(s) serve as the precursor for the synthesis of carnitine in the human body?
narrative: The user wants biochemical information about the amino acid(s) required to produce carnitine, including the metabolic pathway and any dietary relevance. Relevant documents should identify lysine as the primary precursor and explain the role of methionine‑derived methyl groups in the biosynthesis, describe the enzymatic steps, and note whether these amino acids are essential. Documents that merely list essential amino acids without connecting them to carnitine synthesis are not considered relevant.

title: Shebang Definition
description: What is the meaning and origin of the term “shebang” in computing and programming?
narrative: The user seeks a clear explanation of the word “shebang,” including its lexical origin, historical background, and how it is used (e.g., the “#!” line in Unix scripts). Relevant documents should describe the etymology, the slang history, and the technical role of the hash‑bang mechanism in executable scripts.

title: Kraft Sons Count
description: What is the number of sons that Robert Kraft has?
narrative: The user wants to know how many sons Robert Kraft has. Relevant documents should state the total count of his male children and preferably list their names. Any source indicating that he has four sons (Jonathan, Daniel, Joshua, David) meets the information need.

title: Data Analyst Salary
description: What is the average annual income for a data analyst?
narrative: The user wants up‑to‑date compensation information for the data analyst occupation, preferably expressed as average or median annual salary in the United States. Relevant documents should provide numerical salary figures, specify the year or source of the data, and clearly identify the role as a data analyst (or a closely comparable analytical job).

title: Granulation Tissue Initiation
description: What biological processes trigger the formation of granulation tissue in a healing wound?
narrative: The user seeks a clear explanation of the early cellular and molecular events that start granulation tissue development, including the roles of fibroblasts, capillary growth, collagen deposition, and signaling factors. Relevant documents should describe the initial phase of wound healing that leads to the pink, vascularized granulation layer.

title: Hotel vs Motel
description: What are the main differences between hotels and motels?
narrative: The user wants a clear comparison of hotels and motels, covering definitions, typical services offered, pricing trends, typical length of stay, and any other distinguishing characteristics such as location emphasis. Relevant documents should explicitly discuss how hotels and motels differ in these aspects.

title: Strategy vs Model
description: What distinguishes a company's strategy from its business model?
narrative: The user wants a clear explanation of the conceptual and practical differences between a company's strategy (including corporate, business, and functional levels, competitive positioning, and objectives) and its business model (the systematic method of generating revenue and delivering value). Relevant documents should define each term, highlight their distinct purposes, and illustrate how they relate within an organization.

title: Kemet Name Origin
description: Why did the ancient Egyptians call their land Kemet, meaning “Black Land”?
narrative: The user wants a clear explanation of the term Kemet, including its literal translation, why the ancient Egyptians used it to refer to their country, and any geographical or symbolic reasons such as the dark fertile soil along the Nile. Relevant passages must mention that Kemet was the official name, that it translates as “Black Land,” and that the name derives from the black, nutrient‑rich floodplain soil of the Nile.

title: BMT Definition
description: What does BMT stand for in medical terminology and what does it entail?
narrative: The user is looking for a clear, concise definition of the medical abbreviation "BMT," including its full form (bone marrow transplant) and a brief overview of the procedure, purpose, and related concepts. Relevant documents should explicitly define BMT, describe the bone marrow transplantation process, and mention its role in treating diseases or repairing damaged marrow.

title: Etruscan Definition
description: What does the term “Etruscans” refer to, including their origins, culture, and historical significance?
narrative: The user is seeking a clear, encyclopedic definition of the Etruscans. Relevant documents should provide a concise overview of who the Etruscans were—an ancient civilization of central Italy active roughly between the 8th and 3rd centuries BCE—covering their geographic location, language, social and religious practices, artistic contributions, and their role in the development of early Rome. Documents that merely mention the Etruscans in passing (e.g., in relation to specific rituals or later influences) are not sufficient unless they also contain a comprehensive definition or summary.

title: Laudable Definition
description: What is the meaning of the word "laudable" and how is it used?
narrative: The user seeks a clear, concise definition of the adjective 'laudable', including its sense of being worthy of praise or commendation, any related senses (e.g., medical/health usage), and typical usage examples. Relevant results should provide dictionary-style definitions, pronunciation, synonyms, and illustrate the comparative and superlative forms.

title: Muscle-Bone Interaction
description: How do muscles and bones work together to produce movement?
narrative: The user is looking for a detailed explanation of the musculoskeletal mechanism of movement. Relevant information should describe how skeletal muscles attach to bones via tendons, how neural signals trigger muscle contraction, how this contraction generates force that is transmitted to bones, the role of joints as levers, and examples of joint motions. Documents that cover the interaction of muscles and the skeleton, the conversion of chemical energy to tension, and the coordinated action of muscle groups to move the skeleton are considered relevant.

title: Google Docs Autosave
description: Is Google Docs set to automatically save edits, and can this autosave behavior be turned off or controlled?
narrative: The user seeks clarification on whether Google Docs saves changes automatically, whether the feature can be disabled, and what alternatives (e.g., offline mode) exist for managing saving behavior. Relevant documents should describe the automatic saving mechanism, state that it cannot be disabled, and mention offline editing as a way to temporarily avoid online autosave.

title: Mississippi Income Tax
description: Does the state of Mississippi levy a personal income tax?
narrative: The user wants to know whether Mississippi imposes a state-level personal income tax and, if so, what the rate or key provisions are. Relevant documents must clearly state the presence or absence of an income tax in Mississippi, include any applicable tax rates, exemptions, or special rules, and be authoritative (e.g., state statutes or official tax summaries).

title: Dog Day Meaning
description: What does the phrase “dog day afternoon” mean and what is its origin?
narrative: The user seeks a clear definition of the idiom “dog day afternoon,” including its historical or cultural origin and typical usage. Relevant material should explain the meaning of the phrase, trace its etymology (e.g., connections to the “dog days” of summer or related myths), and provide context for how it is used in everyday language.

title: Naturalization Records Access
description: Is information contained in naturalization records publicly accessible?
narrative: The user wants to determine whether naturalization records are considered public documents that can be accessed for genealogical or legal research. Relevant material should explain the public‑availability status of naturalization records, any privacy or legal restrictions, and point to sources (e.g., government archives, genealogy databases) where such records can be obtained.

title: Extraction Time
description: What is the typical duration of a wisdom tooth removal procedure and the immediate recovery period?
narrative: The user wants concrete information on how long it takes to extract a wisdom tooth, including the time required for the actual surgical or dental procedure (per tooth and overall) and any short‑term post‑procedure downtime. Relevant documents should contain specific time estimates for extraction by a dentist or oral surgeon, overall procedure length, and brief recovery guidance (e.g., days off work).

title: Motivational Speaker Earnings
description: What is the typical income and fee range for motivational speakers?
narrative: The user wants concrete monetary figures about what motivational speakers make, covering average annual salaries, typical speaking fees, and how earnings differ by experience, fame, location, and related factors. Relevant documents should present dollar amounts or ranges and explain the variables influencing speaker compensation.

title: Wind Turbine Cost
description: What is the approximate cost to purchase and install a personal wind turbine?
narrative: The user wants total expense information for acquiring and installing a small‑scale (residential or farm‑level) wind turbine, including equipment price, installation fees, and any typical rebates; relevant results should give cost ranges for kW‑rated turbines (e.g., 1.5 kW, 10 kW, 100 kW) and comparative commercial pricing.

title: Button Quail Egg Frequency
description: How often do button quail lay eggs?
narrative: The user wants detailed information on the laying schedule of button quail, including whether they lay year‑round, typical clutch size, seasonal patterns, and recommended management practices such as providing a rest period for the females. Relevant documents should describe natural breeding seasons, how captivity can cause continuous laying, and advice on giving the birds a break to preserve their health.

title: Vanessa Redgrave Age
description: How old is actress Vanessa Redgrave (including her age at death)?
narrative: The user wants to know Vanessa Redgrave's age. Relevant documents should provide authoritative biographical details such as her birth date and, if she is deceased, her death date or a direct statement of her age at death, allowing the age to be determined (e.g., noting she was 79 years old when she died).

title: Suffix -ia meaning
description: What is the meaning and typical usage of the suffix '-ia' in English and scientific terminology?
narrative: The user wants a clear definition of the suffix '-ia', its linguistic origin, and the kinds of concepts it signals (such as conditions, diseases, states, or procedures). Relevant material should explain that '-ia' is a word‑ending that often indicates a condition or disorder, provide etymological background, and give examples like 'hypoxia' to illustrate its application.

title: Caffeine Narcotic Status
description: Is caffeine legally or medically classified as a narcotic, and what evidence supports its classification?
narrative: The user wants authoritative information on whether caffeine is considered a narcotic, including legal schedules, medical definitions, and any evidence of narcotic-like effects or dependence. Relevant documents should discuss caffeine's pharmacological classification, regulatory status (e.g., DEA schedules, international drug conventions), comparisons to true narcotics, and any documented addiction potential or misuse, especially in combination products.

title: Nebraska Hygienist Salary
description: What is the current average (mean) salary for dental hygienists working in Nebraska?
narrative: The user is interested in factual, up‑to‑date compensation information for dental hygienists employed in the state of Nebraska. Relevant documents should provide a clear average or mean yearly salary figure (preferably recent, e.g., within the last 5 years) and may also include salary ranges from specific employers or job postings. Historical figures (e.g., 2006, 2010) are useful only as context; the primary relevance criterion is the provision of an average salary figure that reflects current market conditions.

title: Dress Alteration Cost
description: What is the average cost to alter a wedding dress?
narrative: The user seeks current, typical price ranges for wedding dress alterations, including average dollar amounts, variations based on dress complexity, location (e.g., bridal salon vs. independent seamstress), and common adjustment types (e.g., taking in, sleeve work). Relevant documents should provide numeric cost estimates, explain factors influencing price, and cite recent data (e.g., 2015‑2020).

title: Cholesterol‑Lowering Foods
description: Which foods are most effective for reducing blood cholesterol levels?
narrative: The user wants evidence‑based information about specific foods that can help lower cholesterol, especially those rich in soluble fiber or other nutrients known to reduce LDL cholesterol. Relevant results should list particular food items, explain how they affect cholesterol, and provide supporting nutritional or clinical details.

title: Carvedilol Uses
description: What medical conditions is carvedilol prescribed for?
narrative: The user is seeking information about the therapeutic indications of carvedilol, including the specific cardiovascular and hypertensive conditions it treats (e.g., hypertension, heart failure, post‑myocardial infarction care). Relevant documents should describe the drug class, approved uses, and whether it is used alone or in combination with other medications.

title: Low TSH Meaning
description: What does a low thyroid‑stimulating hormone (TSH) level indicate?
narrative: The user seeks a clear explanation of the clinical significance of a low TSH result, including the conditions it may signal (e.g., hyperthyroidism, pituitary issues), typical causes, and any related health implications. Relevant documents should describe the relationship between TSH levels and thyroid function, outline possible diagnoses associated with low TSH, and provide context for interpreting the test result.

title: Fever Miscarriage Risk
description: Can fever during early pregnancy cause a miscarriage?
narrative: The user seeks medical information about whether having a fever—especially from common causes like urinary tract infections—in the first trimester can lead to miscarriage, including the mechanisms, risk level, and any related symptoms; relevant documents should discuss fever in pregnancy, its causes, and its association with miscarriage or related complications.

title: ALM Definition
description: What does the term "alm" mean or refer to?
narrative: The user is seeking a clear definition or explanation of the word or abbreviation "alm." Relevant results should describe the meaning of "alm" (e.g., its usage as a misspelling of "alms," a term for charitable donations, or as an abbreviation for roles such as almoner). Documents that provide the etymology, context in religious or historical settings, and examples of how the term is used to denote charitable giving or related positions are considered relevant.

title: Statutory Deed Definition
description: What is a statutory deed and what are its legal characteristics?
narrative: The user wants a clear explanation of what a statutory deed is, including the statutory requirements it must satisfy, the promises it contains, and how it differs from other deed types such as warranty or special warranty deeds. Relevant documents should define the deed, cite applicable statutes, describe recording or conveyance rules, and highlight any jurisdiction‑specific nuances.

title: Chronometer Origin
description: What is a chronometer and who invented it?
narrative: The user seeks a concise definition of a chronometer (including its use in marine navigation and watch certification) and the name of the person who first created it. Relevant documents should explain the concept of a chronometer and identify John Harrison as the inventor of the marine chronometer.

title: Mamey Fruit
description: What is the fruit called mamey, including its botanical name, origin, appearance, and culinary uses?
narrative: The user wants a clear definition of mamey, covering its scientific classification (e.g., Pouteria sapota or related species), where it is grown, physical characteristics (size, color, flesh), taste profile, and typical ways it is eaten or prepared. Relevant documents should explicitly mention mamey and provide these details; references to other fruits such as white sapote are not relevant.

title: Hip Implant Metals
description: Which metals are used in modern hip replacement implants?
narrative: The user wants to know the specific metal alloys employed in hip replacement prostheses (e.g., titanium, stainless steel, cobalt‑chromium, etc.). Relevant documents should identify the metal components of the implant's ball and socket, describe their material properties, and may also note the accompanying plastic spacer. Sources that discuss only surgical procedure or non‑metal materials without naming the metals are not relevant.

title: Della Conflict
description: What type of conflict does Della face in O. Henry’s “The Gift of the Magi”?
narrative: The user seeks a literary analysis that classifies Della’s conflict (e.g., internal/man‑versus‑self) and explains the reasoning behind that classification. Relevant content should describe Della’s dilemma, her sacrifice of her prized hair, the emotional tension she experiences, and how this reflects an internal conflict, possibly referencing the story’s irony or overall theme. Documents that explicitly identify or discuss Della’s internal struggle, or that compare it to external factors, satisfy the information need.

title: Bronchiole Tissue
description: What type of tissue composes the bronchioles?
narrative: The user is seeking a concise anatomical description of the tissue composition of bronchioles, including the epithelial lining, any smooth muscle, connective tissue, and supporting structures. Relevant documents should explicitly state the types of cells or tissue layers (e.g., ciliated columnar epithelium, smooth muscle, connective tissue, cartilage if present) that form bronchioles. Information that only describes larger bronchi or general lung anatomy without mentioning bronchiole-specific tissue types is not relevant.

title: Family Feud debut
description: When did the television game show Family Feud first air?
narrative: The user is looking for the original premiere date (year and, if possible, exact date) of the TV series Family Feud, including context such as the network it debuted on and the original host. Relevant documents must contain verifiable information about the show's first broadcast, not just general history or later episodes.

title: Rock Roll Origin
description: When did rock and roll originate?
narrative: The user wants to know the historical beginning of rock and roll, seeking authoritative information that specifies the period (decade or year) when the genre was first recognized, including early landmark recordings and scholarly consensus on its emergence.

title: Shameless Filming Locations
description: Where was the U.S. TV series Shameless filmed?
narrative: The user wants factual details about the production sites of the American version of Shameless. Relevant content should specify that the series was primarily shot in Los Angeles with exterior scenes filmed in Chicago, and may include particular street addresses or landmarks used for the show. Any source confirming these locations satisfies the information need.
